sourceRef: tip:Three common Chapter 33 mistakes to avoid

slides.headline: Avoid These Pitfalls
slides.body: Stay ahead of rework and compliance issues by recognizing common mistakes in Chapter 33.

slides.headline: Incorrect Form Submission
slides.body: Ensure you use the correct VA Form 22-1999 when submitting Chapter 33 applications.
bullets: Submit the form with all required documentation

slides.headline: Missing Eligibility Documentation
slides.body: Verify that all required eligibility documentation is included in the application.

slides.headline: Inaccurate Certification
slides.body: Ensure the certification process accurately reflects the student's status.
bullets: Follow the 85/15 rule for student enrollment

slides.headline: Failing to Submit on Time
slides.body: Ensure all applications are submitted by the required deadlines to avoid delays.
bullets: Keep an updated calendar of deadlines
Submit applications well before the deadline

slides.headline: Lack of Communication
slides.body: Maintain open communication with students and the SAA to prevent misunderstandings.
Learn more about avoiding common Chapter 33 mistakes and other compliance tips by visiting aimilitaryservices.com/waitlist.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: Stay ahead of rework and compliance issues by recognizing common mistakes in Chapter 33. Avoid these pitfalls with our tips and tools designed to help VA School Certifying Officials like you. Link in bio.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is 'Failing to Submit on Time,' as it could benefit from a more specific tip or action item to make it stand out more clearly.
